sections.content: New state 35! Clicking (26, 47) caused 13 cells to change. Let me check what happened.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed
before = last_transition.before_frame.ascii.split('\n')
after = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("=== Changed cells ===")
for r in range(len(after)):
    for c in range(len(after[r])):
        if r < len(before) and c < len(before[r]) and after[r][c] != before[r][c]:
            print(f"  ({r},{c}): {before[r][c]} -> {after[r][c]}")

# Check key colored centers
ascii_grid = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\n=== Key colored centers ===")
for r in range(len(ascii_grid)):
    for c in range(len(ascii_grid[r])):
        ch = ascii_grid[r][c]
        if ch in 'MO':
            print(f"  ({r},{c}): {ch}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Clicking (26, 47) changed the white pixels around the orange diamond to gray. The white pixels at (49-53, 38-42) turned to gray. This is similar to what happened when I clicked the purple square M centers - they moved the purple square and also changed some pixels.
Looking at the image, the orange diamond now has gray pixels inside it instead of white. The orange object is still at (26, 47).
So the pattern seems to be:
1. Click ring center - starts the sequence
2. Click M centers to move objects along paths
3. Click gray diamond O centers to toggle them
4. Click orange diamond center to move orange object
5. Click new M center to change white pixels to gray
Now I should try clicking the purple square M center at (29, 44) again, or maybe click the orange diamond center again.
Actually, looking at the image, I see the orange diamond now has gray pixels inside. Maybe I need to click the orange diamond center again to complete the level.
Let me try clicking (51, 40) again.
